resistant_population: Enterprise IT and legal departments in regulated industries
evidence: GDPR/HIPAA/SOC2 legally require organizations to restrict user data export and execution in ways that conflict with the layered sovereignty priority rule
Disclosing training data provenance exposes vendors to copyright infringement claims; structural legal incentive against genuine compliance with training lineage auditability

resistant_population: Volunteer-maintained and under-resourced open source projects
evidence: Progressive disclosure / stable interfaces / test infrastructure require sustained funding; volunteer projects structurally cannot meet these as hard requirements

resistant_population: Well-resourced corporate actors in standards bodies
evidence: W3C dominated by browser vendors; IETF steered by companies funding full-time participants; ITU captured by nation-states — multi-stakeholder governance displaces rather than solves capture

resistant_population: Independent security researchers and community watchdog organizations
evidence: Parties with expertise to conduct genuine freedom assessments are uncompensated with no enforcement standing; persistent watchdog communities historically do not form without dedicated funding or regulatory mandate; assessment mechanisms without institutional support decay or become compliance theater

resistant_population: Passive platform users (majority of large-platform users)
evidence: Internet forum participation follows 90/9/1 rule; cooperative governance dominated by active minorities in documented cooperative platforms; developer and power-user interests diverge from casual user interests systematically on software platforms
